elements.title: Return of the Mayflower
elements.contents: ... that Franklin D. Roosevelt was so impressed by the companion paintings Return of the Mayflower (pictured) and Surrender of the German Fleet to the Grand Fleet at Scapa Flow that he commissioned smaller versions for his own private collection?

elements.title: Sol Babitz
elements.contents: ... that violinist Sol Babitz, a pioneer in historically informed performance, was twice evicted by the police from meetings of the American Musicological Society?

elements.title: green beer
elements.contents: ... that some green beer is colored with spirulina algae?

elements.title: a Canadian skier
elements.contents: ... that United States interference almost prevented a Canadian skier from competing for Venezuela at the 2026 Winter Olympics?

elements.title: hunt him down in Minecraft
elements.contents: ... that a YouTuber pays cash bounties for his friends to hunt him down in Minecraft?

elements.title: Caroline Jones
elements.contents: ... that Caroline Jones wore different charity clothes every day in 2015 after her mother died?

elements.title: Old Uncle Ned
elements.contents: ... that Stephen Foster's best-selling song "Old Uncle Ned" made little money for him due to the many pirated publications circulating in the marketplace?

elements.title: Pipiet Senja
elements.contents: ... that the author Pipiet Senja likened her proselytization through teaching and writing to spreading a virus?

elements.title: Mataró
elements.contents: ... that the locomotive Mataró was placed on a monument and fell?
